title: AUC-safe regime stack with sigmoid-only calibration
summary: Scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid is strictly monotonic and preserves ranking.
rationale: Scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid is strictly monotonic and preserves ranking. For this competition, that makes regime-specific stacking plus sigmoid calibration more aligned with the metric than generic isotonic post-processing.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 complementary specialists, for example current heuristic GBM, raw-cat CatBoost, and the ranking or latent-state model. Generate race-group OOF predictions, then fit a simple logistic stacker with interaction terms for regime flags such as wet vs slick, early vs late race, and legal-stop-pressure states. Use sigmoid calibration only on the final stack if needed; avoid isotonic unless it is evaluated solely on log loss.
expected_effect: Should convert several small complementary gains into a safer public-LB improvement while preserving the AUC ordering behavior that matters.
risk: Stacking can overfit if the meta-model sees in-fold base predictions or if regimes are too granular for only 26 races. The benefit depends on genuinely diverse specialists, not multiple near-duplicates of the same tree model.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
